context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Left

episode_text:
day=9; meal=cantaloupes, bananas, watermelon; setting=Watching TV with TV on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the baseline reheating time in front-TV savory meals. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish OR bite-sized sandwiches THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected longer reheating in front-TV meals for those more substantial lunch items. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce-bearing, dip, or dessert items served warm THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user explicitly selected longer reheating in observed left-attention meals with such foods. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwaving for a left-TV fruit breakfast, showing that preserving cold temperature overrides the broader left-context tendency toward extra reheating. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cold fruit with whipped cream or another cold-served item THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwaving in the observed right-TV cold-fruit meal, consistent with preserving intended cold temperature.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose front-occlusion minimization in a front-social context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the forward view when an important visual target was directly ahead. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce-bearing, dip, dessert, or cold fruit items THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose left-occlusion minimization whenever the important visual target was on the left, including both complex foods and a simple fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes dessert or other TV-snacking items THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose right-occlusion minimization in a right-TV meal, showing the same view-preservation tendency extends to the right side.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips in simpler savory meals THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected the fastest speed in repeated front-TV meals with straightforward foods. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish THEN medium BECAUSE the user slowed the robot for a messier mixed entree, suggesting more caution for complex bites. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected the slowest speed in that meal, indicating some solid hand-held foods prompt maximum caution. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or cold fruit items THEN slow BECAUSE left-attention contexts now repeatedly show a preference for slower motion, including both complex foods and a simple fruit breakfast, suggesting divided attention on the left drives a comfort-first speed choice. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a light cold dessert/fruit item THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in the observed right-TV strawberries meal, indicating not all dessert contexts require slowing.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering in that meal, suggesting a preference for narrower bites for sandwich pieces. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cut fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering in the observed left-TV fruit meal, indicating a narrower-bite preference can also apply to fruit pieces. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips other than bite-sized sandwiches or cut fruit pieces THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option in repeated front-TV savory meals and also kept it for a sauced mixed dish, reflecting a general preference for acquisition reliability unless a narrower bite seems desirable. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is strawberries with whipped cream THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering for that fruit dessert, so fruit does not uniformly imply parallel skewering.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips in simpler savory meals THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in repeated front-TV savory meals, favoring lower interaction burden when bite acquisition is likely straightforward. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish OR bite-sized sandwiches THEN yes BECAUSE the user requested explicit bite-acquisition confirmation in both of those front-TV meals, indicating extra verification is preferred when bite quality may be less predictable. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items with potentially messier or less predictable acquisition THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly requested confirmation in those observed left-attention meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in the observed left-TV fruit meal, showing that left-side divided attention alone does not require confirmation when acquisition seems straightforward. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple fruit-and-cream dessert THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in the observed right-TV meal, suggesting confirmation is reserved for more failure-prone acquisition contexts rather than all desserts.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, so outside-mouth transfer is not physically compatible.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips in simpler savory meals THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only signaling in repeated front-TV meals of that type. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish OR bite-sized sandwiches THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a more salient multimodal cue in both of those front-TV meals, suggesting added prompting when the meal is more complex or requires more careful transfer timing. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or cold fruit items THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected multimodal readiness cues in left-attention contexts, including a simple fruit breakfast, suggesting a preference for more salient signaling when attention is directed leftward. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple fruit-and-cream dessert THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only signaling in the observed right-TV meal, indicating multimodal cues are not always needed when the task feels straightforward.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly selected low-effort automated initiation in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected automated initiation in front-attention meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or other more complex items THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user also selected automated initiation in those divided-attention meals, including the left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a fruit dessert THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automated initiation in the observed right-TV meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE physical button use is incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis. No context-dependent variation beyond this observed yet.

IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly selected automated initiation for drinking in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected automated initiation in front-TV meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or other more complex items THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user also selected automated initiation in those divided-attention meals, including the left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a fruit dessert THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automated initiation in the observed right-TV meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE physical button use is incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis. No context-dependent variation beyond this observed yet.

IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly selected automated initiation for mouth wiping in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected automated initiation in front-TV meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or other more complex items THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user also selected automated initiation in those divided-attention meals, including the left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a fruit dessert THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automated initiation in the observed right-TV meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE physical button use is incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis. No context-dependent variation beyond this observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips in simpler savory meals THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only completion signaling in repeated front-TV meals of that type. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish OR bite-sized sandwiches THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a more salient multimodal completion cue in both of those front-TV meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or cold fruit items THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected multimodal completion cues in left-attention contexts, including the left-TV fruit breakfast, suggesting a preference for more salient signaling when attention is directed away from the robot. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple fruit-and-cream dessert THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only completion signaling in the observed right-TV meal.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN perception BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred reliable automatic detection for bite completion. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected perceptual completion detection in front-TV meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, or other more complex items THEN perception BECAUSE the user also selected automatic perceptual detection in those meals, including the left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a fruit dessert THEN perception BECAUSE the user again selected perceptual completion detection in the observed right-TV meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE physical button use is incompatible. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips in simpler savory meals THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected perceptual completion detection for drinking in repeated front-TV meals of that type. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly selected automated completion for drinking in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish OR bite-sized sandwiches THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated completion in those front-TV meals, suggesting more complex meals shift this task toward lower-effort automation. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or cold fruit items THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated completion in all observed left-attention contexts, including the left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple fruit-and-cream dessert THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected perceptual completion in the observed right-TV meal, showing that this task can revert to perception in a straightforward right-TV dessert context. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE physical button use is incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips in simpler savory meals THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected perceptual completion detection for mouth wiping in repeated front-TV meals of that type. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user explicitly selected automated completion for mouth wiping in that context. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish OR bite-sized sandwiches THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated completion in those front-TV meals, suggesting a shift toward lower-effort automation when the meal is messier or otherwise more complex. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or cold fruit items THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated completion in all observed left-attention contexts, including the left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple fruit-and-cream dessert THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected perceptual completion in the observed right-TV meal, showing that this task can also revert to perception in a straightforward right-TV dessert context. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not button BECAUSE physical button use is incompatible with the user's severe upper-limb paralysis.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in repeated front-TV meals, indicating willingness to accept longer meal time to preserve visibility of a forward visual target. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, or cold fruit items THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retracting in left-attention contexts, including the left-TV fruit breakfast, indicating a stable willingness to accept longer meal time to preserve visibility of an important left-side target. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a fruit dessert THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in the observed right-TV meal, extending the same visibility-preservation tendency to right-side visual targets.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no sauces or dips AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish without a separate dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for that entree, indicating that sauce on the food does not imply a desire for added dipping. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown THEN less BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred light dipping rather than heavy dipping in observed sauce-containing left-attention meals. IF food is strawberries with whipped cream or another lightly dippable dessert AND dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown THEN less BECAUSE the user selected light dipping in the observed right-TV fruit dessert meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit without dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping in the observed left-TV fruit breakfast.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food has no sauces or dips THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in repeated front-TV savory meals, including the sandwich meal. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that front-TV sauced meal, indicating more time to intervene is preferred when the meal is messier or more complex. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left OR working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, or dessert items that divide attention or may need intervention THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user explicitly selected a very long wait in those observed left-attention meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in the observed left-TV fruit breakfast, showing that left-side divided attention alone does not force a long timeout when the meal is straightforward. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple fruit-and-cream dessert THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait in the observed right-TV meal, suggesting long waits are reserved for more demanding or intervention-prone situations rather than all desserts.
